message: Parameter tuning has stalled. The Gemini agents have been unable to improve the composite score for 3 iterations.

checks.name: temperature_range
checks.reason: Temperatures must stay within physically realizable ocean range

checks.name: equator_pole_gradient
checks.reason: Meridional temperature gradient must exist and be reasonable

checks.name: hemispheric_symmetry
checks.reason: Ocean temperatures are roughly symmetric; large asymmetry suggests broken forcing

checks.name: deep_colder_than_surface
checks.value: 1/15 bands have deep warmer than surface
checks.reason: Deep ocean is always colder than surface (stable stratification)

checks.name: western_intensification
checks.reason: Stommel theory: beta effect concentrates return flow on western boundary

checks.name: subtropical_gyre_exists
checks.reason: Wind-driven subtropical gyre must develop from Sverdrup balance

checks.name: southern_ocean_flow
checks.reason: Drake Passage is open — ACC should develop from wind stress + no land barrier

checks.name: deep_water_formation_signature
checks.reason: Thermohaline circulation: cold dense water sinks at poles, fills deep ocean

recentHistory.hypothesis: Complete failure of western intensification and AMOC-driven overturning circulation.
Complete collapse of western intensification and AMOC-driven overturning circulation

physicistLastHypothesis.dominant_error: Complete collapse of western intensification and AMOC-driven overturning circulation

hypotheses.mechanism: The beta-effect or wind-stress curl coupling is insufficient to overcome the current lateral viscosity (A_visc), preventing the formation of tight western boundary currents and suppressing the poleward heat transport required for AMOC.
hypotheses.side_effects: Lowering viscosity will likely increase numerical noise and may require a corresponding adjustment to bottom friction to maintain stability.

hypotheses.mechanism: The thermohaline coupling (gamma_deep_form) is too weak to create the density gradient necessary to drive deep water formation, resulting in a stagnant deep ocean and zero AMOC.
hypotheses.side_effects: Increased deep water formation will accelerate the global conveyor belt, potentially leading to excessive cooling of the deep ocean basins if not balanced by surface heat flux.

hypotheses.mechanism: The bottom friction (r_friction) is too high, acting as a drag on the entire flow field and preventing the development of the high-velocity jets characteristic of western boundary currents.
hypotheses.side_effects: Reduced bottom friction will likely increase the overall kinetic energy of the system and could lead to unrealistic current speeds in the ocean interior.

physicistLastHypothesis.recommended: H1 — The total lack of western intensification (ratio 0.0x) suggests that the dynamics are being overly damped by lateral viscosity, preventing the formation of the boundary currents that drive the global circulation.

suggestion: Consider whether structural changes to the physics code (index.html) are needed — new forcing terms, different boundary conditions, modified advection scheme — rather than just parameter tuning.
